comment: "Currently I am working as a nurse in Rehabilitative center in Thailand, we have to provide rehabilitative and intermediate nursing care for severely burn patients (60%) after discharged. Calories calculation in this course is the most interesting part for me. The topic, content and objective are closely aligned with my current job."

"I found the most interesting aspects of this course to be the comprehensive approach to burn patient care. The discussions on the hypermetabolic response and the importance of nutrition were particularly valuable, as they highlighted the systemic impact of burns beyond the skin. I also enjoyed learning about the complexity of pain mechanisms in burn patients and how multidisciplinary strategies can improve both survival and quality of life."

"The aspect of the course that I found most interesting was the lecture delivered by Nurse Susan Smith. It was amazing. I learned a lot about burns, wound healing, and how to help a patient overcome the different types of pain they experience. and the nutritional requirements for quick wound healing."
"I am an advanced life support paramedic from Zambia and we never learnt burns to this depth, this has been very helpful."

"The most interesting part of this course is pain management being considered in various aspects of the patient beyond the wound/burn site. Most of the health care providers in LMIC focus on the wound than the patient as a whole which greatly delays wound healing, an emotional damage and other psychological challenges."

"I liked the approach of teaching and the pace. This has helped me appreciate the concepts around the appropriate management of pain, as this is an essential component ensuring effective burn wound treatment and optimizes patient participation and comfort too. Also, this has further helped me appreciate the fact that post operative dressings must be secure to protect skin grafts and finally, the aspect of nutritional requirements in supporting healing must always be considered."
"Thank you very much for making this course available. Burns is such a common and destructive trauma in my country, and it is not taught in depth at medical and nursing school."
